_units: median wall-clock in milliseconds from pytest-benchmark default config (warmup absorbed)
_notes: Post-T30 Step 3 baseline. The cumsum FFI in `crates/polars-metal-mlx-sys` now passes input/output as `rust::Slice` (thin pointer+length) instead of per-element `cxx::CxxVector` push/iter loops. With 10M-row inputs the Rust-side push of 10M u8s plus the iter-copy of 10M u32s was costing ~40ms per call on its own; eliminating those loops drops filter_simple from 117.2ms to 77.8ms (-34%) and brings the Metal/CPU ratio for filter_simple under 9x for the first time. The internal MLX `array(ptr, shape, dtype)` constructor still copies bytes into MLX-managed memory and we memcpy the scan result back into the caller's slice — those two memcpys are the only data-touching passes left in the cumsum call. Pushing the FFI cost lower than this requires the M2 MLX-over-MTLBuffer rewrite tracked in docs/open-questions.md.

tpch_q1_modified._notes: ⚠ CPU-vs-CPU measurement, not Metal. Investigation 2026-05-27 found the walker falls back at the Sort IR node for every Q1 query, so the Metal engine is a no-op for all `tpch_q1_*` benches. Both `engine='cpu'` and `engine=MetalEngine()` execute the identical CPU plan; the ~1% delta is noise. Real M3 Metal work is at the kernel layer (Phase 4.5: A1 30ms vs CPU 130ms at 10M × 1024 groups in microbench) but doesn't reach this engine-level query path until the walker handles Sort + Filter→GroupBy fusion (deferred to M4 — initial attempt caused a 600× regression).

tpch_q1_modified_32bit._notes: ⚠ CPU-vs-CPU — same caveat as tpch_q1_modified. Walker fallback at Sort.

tpch_q1_modified_32bit_high_card._notes: ⚠ CPU-vs-CPU — Walker falls back at Sort. The 18% delta is likely noise from pytest-benchmark's variance at this measurement scale (CPU = 94.97 ± 23.22 IQR per the original bench output). Retracted as a Metal win.

tpch_q1_canonical._notes: ⚠ CPU-vs-CPU — walker falls back at Sort (Utf8 keys don't change that). Phase 7 (KeyDtype::Utf8) IS shipped and verified correct in-kernel + via test_string_groupby integration tests; this canonical-Q1 bench just doesn't exercise it because the Sort-at-root fallback prevents the engine from engaging. M4 work to handle Sort in the walker is the prerequisite for canonical Q1 becoming a real Metal-vs-CPU measurement.
